name: hunting-lodge-large
summary: A substantial timber lodge set in a private hunting preserve. Great hearth, trophy heads on the walls, tanning yard behind, dog kennels and a gear-porch. A noble's rustic pleasure, staffed year-round.

primary_palette.note: the hall's hearth — trophy-lined wall, long table facing the fire
feast table along the hall, trophies and weapons displayed
yard side of the lodge — processing game, stretched hides

story_prompts: noble in residence with full retinue — feast night, dogs in the kennel, guards posted
off-season caretaker only — lodge closed, kennels empty, the ranger sleeps in the hall
the noble died on the hunt — the retinue is searching, the lodge is in disarray
a rival claim — two parties insist the lodge is theirs, one forged the deed
something in the preserve is hunting back — the trophy wall has a new kind of skull on it

lighting_notes.ambient_intent: interior lodge — warm firelit hall, daylit yard through open doors

anti_patterns: this one DOES have built walls — a lodge is a building, not a camp
keep the yard outdoor — dirt, not floorboards, past the porch line
trophies belong on the wall, not stacked on the floor — display, not storage
do not make the hall feel like a castle — timbers and antlers, not tapestries and marble
